title: astragalus sibiricus
intro: Astragalus sibiricus, also known as Huangqi, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and the Himalayas. It is a member of the legume family and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its adaptogenic and medicinal properties. The plant's roots contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, providing health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced stress levels. Astragalus sibiricus can be prepared in various forms, including capsules, tablets, teas, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine formulas to support overall well-being.
